creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Po-Chuan Hsieh from comment #11)

I do not see any issue in portmaster, but in the dependencies declared by the port.

The "stage" phase occurs before the program can be run.
If there is a dependency that needs to be provided for the "stage" phase, then this is a build dependency.

Portmaster provides BUILD_DEPENDS before the build of a port is started.
It updates and installs RUN_DEPENDS after the port requiring them has been packaged (if requested) and installed.

Expecting RUN_DEPENDS to be provided before the "stage" phase is wrong, IMHO.
It works due to the ordering of targets in bsd.port.mk, but this is an artifact of the missing persistent dependency tracking it provides.

If bsd.port.mk performed the installation of RUN_DEPENDS after the port being built, then it could come into a situation where a run dependency fails, but since the installation of the port has already succeeded, another "make install" in the port's directory will see that the port itself has been installed and will stop and not try to install the missing run dependencies.

This causes RUN_DEPENDS to be installed before the install phase of the port begins.

Portmaster expects BUILD_DEPENDS to cover everything required to build, stage, and package a port. It expects RUN_DEPENDS to cover everything actually required to run a package (i.e., the same dependencies as processed by the pkg command when installing a package).

This treatment of dependencies prevents dependency recursions. It would be trivial to treat RUN_DEPENDS as BUILD_DEPENDS, but this will create dependency loops.

There is no reason that prevents adding BUILD_DEPENDS to ports that require some other port to be present during the package, stage, or install phases. These are all phases that come before a port can be run, so please add the BUILD_DEPENDS entry.

It seems that poudriere does actually provide RUN_DEPENDS before executing "make stage". This mimics the behavior of bsd.port.mk and has probably been implemented to work around missing BUILD_DEPENDS entries. This is easily possible in poudriere, since it installs all dependencies from packages.

Portmaster cannot easily do the same, since it installs build dependencies by directly building them and installing them on the base system, when they are needed for the first time. But the solution is trivial: add BUILD_DEPENDS, where a dependency is required to perform any of the stages required to build a port.

If you want to experiment with RUN_DEPENDS being treated like BUILD_DEPENDS, I can easily provide a patch that implements that logic. But then, there might be dependency loops that are very hard or impossible to break, in contrast to the always trivial definition of BUILD_DEPENDS in the ports that are affected by the issue being reported.
